mnemonics: PAN Loves Very Close Connections (Personal Area, LAN, VLAN, CAN, Campus Area, WAN)
keyPoints: A PAN (Personal Area Network) connects devices within an individual's workspace.
A LAN (Local Area Network) connects devices within a limited geographic area like a building.
A WAN (Wide Area Network) spans a large geographic area, often connecting multiple LANs.
commonMistakes: Confusing VLAN (Virtual LAN) with WAN, as VLAN is a logical segmentation within a physical LAN.
Assuming CAN stands for Controller Area Network (used in vehicles) instead of Campus Area Network.
